top10.introHtml: Top 10 best-value convertibles within 35 miles of Media, PA — ranked for a cheap fun second car. Condition and reliability over mileage; manual transmission preferred.

picks.whyGood: Mazda Miata is the gold standard cheap fun convertible — legendary reliability, parts everywhere, and a driver-focused chassis that rewards spirited driving. 82K miles on a Miata is nothing; these motors routinely go past 200K. Touring trim means Bilstein shocks and 16-inch alloys. No-accident verified by AutoTrader.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top through a full cycle — check for tears, cloudy rear window plastic, and water stains on carpet behind seats. Inspect rocker panels and door sills for surface rust (common on older Miatas). Confirm timing belt has been replaced (required every 60K on 1.8L; this is a 1.8L car). Check for any clunk in the rear diff on takeoff.
picks.bottomLine: Best bang-for-buck fun convertible in this search — buy it.

picks.whyGood: GXP means the 260hp turbocharged 2.0L Ecotec — this is a genuinely fast car for the money. Clean title and no-accident verified on Cars.com, Great Deal rating, and 88K miles is very reasonable for a 2009. The Solstice GXP is essentially a Kappa platform sports car with sports-car power for $8K.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the soft top fully — the Solstice top is manual and the rear window is plastic; check for delamination and zipper integrity. Inspect drain tubes at each corner of the top well (prone to clogging, causing wet carpet). Check for boost leaks and intercooler piping condition. Confirm clutch engagement feels crisp (manual preferred check).
picks.bottomLine: 260hp turbo roadster for $8,195 with a Great Deal rating — outstanding value if top and drains are solid.

picks.whyGood: The E88 128i is one of the most satisfying naturally-aspirated BMW convertibles — a 3.0L inline-six with a proper hydraulic soft top, RWD, and last of the analog-feel BMWs. 69K miles is low for a 2013 at $9,500, and it appears at both AutoTrader and CarGurus at the same price/mileage from B&D Auto Sales — consistent listing is reassuring.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the hydraulic soft top through a full cycle — listen for hydraulic pump strain and check for hesitation mid-cycle indicating a low fluid level or leaking ram. Check headliner for water staining. Inspect all four roof drain tubes. Test cooling system (N52 engine can develop coolant leaks at the reservoir and expansion tank). Request service records for oil changes.
picks.bottomLine: Best BMW in the budget at a genuinely low mileage — the E88 128i is an underrated driver's convertible.

picks.whyGood: E46 325Ci Convertible with only 60K miles is a unicorn find. The E46 is widely considered BMW's last great analog convertible — balanced chassis, M54 inline-six, real hydraulic top. Great Deal rating at $10K. Mancini Motors is a Norristown independent with a long reputation. 60K miles on a 23-year-old car means this was likely a weekend/second car.
picks.whatToVerify: Hydraulic top: full cycle, check reservoir under trunk liner for fluid level and leaks. Check for cooling system leaks (E46 M54 plastic coolant components age and crack — inspect expansion tank, water pump, thermostat housing). Check brake fluid condition. Inspect rear subframe mounting points for rust (known E46 issue on high-mileage cars, less likely at 60K). Oil condition on dipstick.
picks.bottomLine: 60K-mile E46 325Ci at $10K with Great Deal — if the cooling system is solid, this is a strong buy.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 1.9 is the lightest, most tossable version — 138hp but only 2,700 lbs means it punches above its weight on back roads. 69K miles is very low. Only 9.4 miles from Media makes this the closest interesting roadster on the list. Dougherty Automotive Services in West Chester is a well-regarded independent BMW shop. No-accident verified.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top: check for tears, rear window clarity, and whether the top seals properly at highway speed (water test or close inspection of door seals). M43 1.9L is simple and bulletproof — check for any oil leaks around valve cover. Inspect for rust in wheel wells and under rocker panels. Confirm HVAC works (blend door actuators on older Z3s can fail).
picks.bottomLine: 9 miles away, 69K miles, in a reputable BMW shop's inventory — an easy test drive from Media.

picks.whyGood: 51K miles on a Solstice GXP is impressively low — this car has been well kept. The turbocharged 2.0L Ecotec makes this one of the fastest cars in the sub-$15K convertible market. Price-dropped listing from Automotive Avenues of Sewell, a established NJ dealer.
picks.whatToVerify: Same Solstice GXP checks: operate the soft top, inspect drain channels, boost system integrity. At 51K miles the top should be in good shape. Check for any rust on the hard sill panels under the doors — the Kappa cars can accumulate moisture there. Verify the turbo spools cleanly under load.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mileage GXP at a solid price — the extra $3K over the Pennsauken GXP buys 37K fewer miles.

picks.whyGood: The R170 SLK 230 is a tight, direct sports roadster with a retractable hardtop — meaning no soft-top rot issues. The supercharged 2.3L Kompressor is reliable and responsive. 68K miles and clean/no-accident on Cars.com. Only 10 miles from Media and a Good Deal rating.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the retractable hardtop (R170 mechanism is generally reliable but test full cycle; listen for straining motors). Check for supercharger whine beyond normal. Inspect for coolant leaks around the reservoir. Check the key fob and convertible top switch operation. Look for any electrical gremlins (window regulators are a known weak point on early 2000s Mercedes).
picks.bottomLine: Retractable hardtop means no weather worries — solid urban/suburban fun car at $9K.

picks.whyGood: The budget pick: $7,500 Great Deal on a base Solstice with 63K miles. The 177hp 2.4L Ecotec in the base Solstice is naturally aspirated and simple to maintain. 63K miles is quite good. Great Deal rated by CarGurus.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top operation and rear window condition (same as GXP). Look specifically at the top's fabric condition — budget Solstices sometimes have deferred maintenance on the top. Check drain channels. Inspect brake rotor condition. Confirm no CEL.
picks.bottomLine: The cheapest fun roadster on the list with Great Deal status — perfect if you just want a summer toy.

picks.whyGood: The E85 Z4 2.5i is the sharper, more driver-focused successor to the Z3 — better chassis, better steering, same bulletproof inline-six. 67K miles is very clean. Being listed at Thompson BMW in Doylestown (a franchised BMW dealer) means it's gone through their service inspection. No-accident verified.
picks.whatToVerify: Hydraulic soft top: full cycle, listen for pump lag. Check for rust around the top well drainage points. The Z4 E85 has similar cooling system plastic component aging to the E46 — inspect expansion tank and reservoir. Confirm the window regulator operation (both sides). Thompson BMW service history would be ideal to request.
picks.bottomLine: A franchised BMW dealer-sourced Z4 at 67K miles for under $10,500 — excellent credibility.

picks.whyGood: Same 2009 Solstice GXP as rank 2 (same mileage, same Pennsauken location) — listed on CarGurus at $8,590 vs $8,195 on Cars.com. Useful as a second source to verify the listing. Great Deal on CarGurus independently confirms the value.
picks.whatToVerify: Same checks as rank 2. Compare both listings closely before contacting — this may be the same physical car cross-listed, in which case use the Cars.com listing price of $8,195 as leverage.
picks.bottomLine: Independent Great Deal confirmation for the Solstice GXP — or use it as price leverage on the $8,195 listing.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles found within 35–42 miles of Media, PA in budget. V8 GT trims ranked first.

picks.whyGood: SN-95 GT with only 44K original miles — extraordinarily low for a 1994. The 5.0 V8 (215hp, torquey and tuneable) makes this the most engaging Mustang convertible in the budget. 44K miles suggests a stored or very lightly used example. Clean title verified on Cars.com.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible-specific: inspect the top mount-to-rocker panel seam for rust (the most common structural issue on these body styles). Check frame rails under the engine bay. Operate the manual top and inspect all seals. Look at rear seat for water staining. Start cold and listen for lifter clatter. Confirm 5.0L badge (not the 3.8L V6 GT mis-titled).
picks.bottomLine: 44K-mile SN-95 GT is the unicorn Mustang of this search — if structurally sound, buy it immediately.

picks.whyGood: Another low-mileage SN-95 GT — 55K miles and no-accident verified. GT Motors is a specialist dealer in Philadelphia. The 5.0L GT is a strong naturally-aspirated V8 that responds well to simple maintenance.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 checks as rank 1. Also test the T-5 manual transmission shift quality — 3rd gear synchro can wear at high mileage but 55K should be fine. Inspect convertible top seals around rear quarters for shrinkage cracking.
picks.bottomLine: Clean SN-95 GT from a specialist dealer — the GT nameplate and 55K miles justify the $500 premium over the Eddington car.

picks.whyGood: S197 V6 with 64K miles and a Great Deal rating. The 3.7L V6 in 2013 makes 305hp — more than the old GT V8 — and gets decent fuel economy. Clean title and no-accidents. Bordentown is 42 miles out but for a Great Deal Mustang it's worth the drive.
picks.whatToVerify: S197 convertible checks: top operation through full cycle, water stain inspection on rear seat and trunk carpeting (common leak point around rear window seam). Test all electronics. Inspect rear subframe mounting points. The V6 is generally trouble-free — check for any check engine light history.
picks.bottomLine: Modern 305hp V6 Mustang convertible under $11K with Great Deal status — the practical choice.
